motions.title: Tarnbrick Farm Green Waste Composting Application
motions.full_text: That planning permission be granted subject to the conditions set out in Appendix 'A' for planning application LCC/2025/0021: Change of use of land from agriculture to green waste composting (part retrospective) together with construction of new internal access road, construction of new building for a workshop and processing and extension of new building for storage of finished product - Tarnbrick Farm, Blackpool Road, Kirkham, PR4 2RE.
motions.opposition_strategy: Opposition will likely focus on the 'part retrospective' nature of the application, arguing it sets a poor precedent or indicates a lack of prior control. They may also raise concerns about local amenity impacts such as noise, dust, traffic, and visual intrusion, despite the proposed conditions. County Councillor Rick Edwards (Reform UK) is on the committee and may speak on general development control principles, though not from the immediate area.
motions.ruling_group_response: Emphasise that the application brings existing, partially retrospective operations under formal planning control, ensuring future compliance. Highlight that the proposed conditions (e.g., operating hours, white noise alarms, dust control, sheeting vehicles) are robust and designed to mitigate any impacts on local residents and the environment. Stress the economic benefits of the facility and its role in green waste recycling, aligning with environmental policy. Note officer recommendation for approval based on policy compliance.

amendment_predictions.likely_proposer: County Councillor R Edwards (Reform UK) or other opposition member
amendment_predictions.amendment_angle: Propose stricter conditions on operating hours, noise levels, or traffic management, or seek a deferral for further environmental assessment.
amendment_predictions.counter: Argue that existing conditions are sufficient and proportionate. If a minor, non-substantive amendment (e.g., minor wording change to a condition) is proposed, consider accepting it as a friendly amendment to demonstrate flexibility and save debate time. Reject amendments that fundamentally undermine the recommendation or are not supported by evidence.

key_data_points: Part retrospective change of use of 1.8 hectares of land
New building (60.9m x 24.3m x 10m height) and extension (18.2m x 12.4m x 9.6m height)
11 planning conditions proposed, including specific operating hours (0800-1800 Mon-Fri, 0800-1200 Sat/Sun/PH) and white noise reversing alarms.

motions.title: Harwood Lane Children's Care Home Application
motions.full_text: That planning permission be granted subject to the conditions set out in Appendix 'A' for planning application LCC/2025/0024: A change of use from class C3 (dwelling house) to Class C2 (residential institutions) to enable the property to be used as a home for up to two young persons between the ages of 11 and 17 years with no more than two carers staying in the property overnight providing care as required - 9 Harwood Lane, Great Harwood.
motions.opposition_strategy: Opposition, particularly County Councillors Ashley Joynes (Reform UK, Accrington South) and Mr Joel Michael Tetlow (Reform UK, Accrington North) who represent nearby Hyndburn divisions, will likely amplify the three letters of objection received. Arguments will focus on potential negative impacts on residential amenity, character of the area, increased traffic, parking issues, and perceived safety concerns. They may question the 'need' or 'suitability' of the specific location.
motions.ruling_group_response: Strongly emphasise the critical need for accommodation for looked after children, citing national policy (NPPF and 'Planning for accommodation for looked after children' statement) which encourages such provision. Highlight the small scale of the proposal (up to two children, two overnight carers, up to four day staff on rota) and the existing off-street parking for six vehicles, mitigating traffic/parking concerns. Stress that key consultees (Hyndburn BC, LCC Highways) raised no objections. Point to condition 3, which specifically limits the use to a children's care home for up to two children, preventing intensification. Reassure that the facility will be managed professionally.

amendment_predictions.likely_proposer: County Councillor A Joynes (Reform UK) or County Councillor K Snape (Labour & Co-operative)
amendment_predictions.amendment_angle: Propose additional conditions relating to staff numbers, visitor hours, or a review period for the permission, or seek a deferral for a community impact assessment.
amendment_predictions.counter: Argue that the proposed conditions are sufficient and proportionate, and that further restrictions could undermine the viability or purpose of the home. Explain that the management plan (already submitted) addresses operational details. Deferral is unnecessary given no objections from statutory consultees. If a minor, procedural amendment is proposed, consider accepting as friendly to show collaboration.

key_data_points: Change of use from C3 (dwelling) to C2 (residential institution)
Home for up to two young persons (11-17 years old)
Up to two carers overnight, up to four staff during the day (rota system)
Detached house in residential area with private garden and off-street parking for 6 vehicles
LCC Highways Development Control: No objections
Condition 3 limits use strictly to a children's care home for two children, preventing other C2 uses.

questions.topic: Harwood Lane Children's Care Home - Local Impact
questions.question_text: Given the concerns raised by local residents regarding the proposed children's care home at 9 Harwood Lane, how can the Committee be sure that this facility will not negatively impact the quiet residential character of the area, particularly concerning noise, parking, and safety for existing families?
recommended_answer_points: Reiterate the low occupancy (up to two children) and the professional management of the home.
Highlight the ample off-street parking (6 spaces) to mitigate parking concerns.
Emphasise that the facility is for children in need of care, not a disruptive institution, and will operate within a residential context.
Refer to the planning conditions, particularly condition 3, which limits the use specifically to a two-child care home, preventing any future intensification without further planning review.
Mention that LCC Highways found no issues with traffic or parking.
questions.trap_explanation: This question seeks to exploit local anxieties and 'Not In My Backyard' sentiment. It could lead to emotional appeals and attempts to paint the facility as a threat to community safety or amenity. The trap is to get drawn into a debate about the 'type' of children or to over-promise on absolute guarantees, rather than sticking to planning merits and conditions.

tactical_scenarios.scenario: Opposition attempts to defer a planning application for further information or a site visit.
tactical_scenarios.counter: The ruling group should argue that all necessary information is before the committee, and officers have provided a comprehensive assessment with a clear recommendation. A deferral would cause unnecessary delay and cost. Only agree if a genuinely new and material planning consideration is raised that cannot be addressed by conditions. Use the Chair to keep debate focused on the current report.
tactical_scenarios.standing_order: Motion debate time limits (30 min per motion)

tactical_scenarios.scenario: An opposition member proposes a recorded vote on a contentious planning application.
tactical_scenarios.counter: This is a standard opposition tactic to highlight divisions and put members on record. The ruling group should be prepared for this. Ensure all ruling group members are present and understand the rationale for supporting the officer recommendation. A recorded vote itself does not consume much time, but the preceding debate might be prolonged.
tactical_scenarios.standing_order: Not explicitly covered in provided SOs, but common practice allows members to request a recorded vote.

tactical_scenarios.scenario: Debate on a motion runs close to the 30-minute limit, risking the item not being disposed of.
tactical_scenarios.counter: The Chair must strictly enforce time limits for speakers (Mover: 5 min, Others: 3 min). If debate is becoming repetitive, the Chair should intervene. Consider using a friendly amendment (if one is viable and beneficial) to quickly incorporate a minor point and move to a vote, potentially shortening further debate. The critical rule about motions not disposed of at Full Council falling implies a strong need to conclude items at this meeting.
tactical_scenarios.standing_order: Motion debate: 30 min per motion, Mover: 5 min, Others: 3 min

time_management.recommended_time_saving: Strictly enforce speaker time limits (3 min for 'Others'). Utilise friendly amendments on motions if minor changes can satisfy some opposition concerns without significant debate. The Chair should actively manage debate to prevent repetition, particularly on the Harwood Lane item.

media_risk_items: Harwood Lane Children's Care Home application could generate local press interest due to resident objections and the sensitive nature of the proposal.

standing_orders_summary.friendly_amendment_rule: Motion mover may amend their own motion or accept an amendment without debate or vote
standing_orders_summary.motions_that_fall_rule: Any motion not disposed of at Full Council falls. Does not carry forward to next meeting.

archived.summary: Politically loaded meeting. 4 notices of motion (constitutional maximum), 5 member questions, 2 public questions. Key battlegrounds: potholes motion directly targets Reform election promise; care homes questions test day centre commitment; highways budget question from Conservative leader.
